Add-on Treatment.evidence: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patientsstarting hormone therapy: 5-year follow-up results from theSTAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)
Add-on Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone". This is explicitly stated in the title of the article on page 1, in the abstract on page 2, and in the "Procedures" section (2.3) on page 4.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity.
Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of patients experiencing all-cause adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) is reported as 80 (16%). This is derived from the text on page 6, "Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity." The total number of patients in the SOC + AAP group is 501 (Table 1, page 5), so 16% of 501 is approximately 80 patients. While "all-cause" is not explicitly stated, "toxicity" in this general context typically refers to all-cause.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity.
Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of patients experiencing all-cause adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the control arm (SOC-alone) is reported as 80 (16%). This is derived from the text on page 6, "Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity." The total number of patients in the SOC-alone group is 502 (Table 1, page 5), so 16% of 502 is approximately 80 patients. While "all-cause" is not explicitly stated, "toxicity" in this general context typically refers to all-cause.

The document reports "grade 3 or higher toxicity" (page 6) and "worst reported grade of toxicity" (page 8), but does not specify if these adverse events were "treatment-related". Therefore, this specific information is not reported.

The document states "no grade 4 or 5 toxicity" for the 2-year data (page 8), which would imply no grade 5 events. However, this statement refers to "worst reported grade of toxicity" (not specified as treatment-related) and is for the 2-year follow-up. The 4-year data on page 6 mentions "grade 3 or higher toxicity" but does not specify grade 5 or treatment-related. Therefore, specific treatment-related grade 5 data is not reported.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.plan_extraction_plan: The PDF describes the trial design, statistical methods, and results, but it does not provide an explicit overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) for the randomized controlled trial (RCT) itself. This type of assessment, often related to bias or quality using specific tools, is not reported.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: While those agents [apalutamide and enzalutamide] are androgen receptor antagonists, all three drugs [apalutamide, enzalutamide, and abiraterone] work by targeting the androgen receptor axis...this extended analysis further reinforces the body of data on the substantial benefits of upfront targeting of the androgen receptor pathway using abiraterone acetate
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.chunk_id: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1::1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.plan_extraction_plan: The treatment arm 1 is "SOC + AAP" (Standard-of-care plus Abiraterone Acetate and Prednisolone). The experimental agent added is Abiraterone Acetate. On page 10, in the "DISCUSSION" section (Chunk 7), the text states: "While those agents [apalutamide and enzalutamide] are androgen receptor antagonists, all three drugs [apalutamide, enzalutamide, and abiraterone] work by targeting the androgen receptor axis." The conclusion on the same page also mentions "upfront targeting of the androgen receptor pathway using abiraterone acetate". Therefore, the class of agent for abiraterone acetate is an androgen receptor axis targeting agent.

Control Arm.plan_extraction_plan: The control arm is referred to as "SOC-alone" or "SOC" (Standard-of-Care). The abstract on page 3 states: "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy."

Control Arm - N.plan_extraction_plan: The total number of participants in the control arm (SOC-alone) is 502. This can be extracted from: Page 5, Table 1, "SOC-alone n = 502" row. Page 5, Figure 1 (CONSORT diagram), "Allocated to SOC N = 502" box. Page 6, Table 2, "SOC-alone n = 502" row. Page 4, Text, Section 3 "RESULTS", first paragraph: "Of these, all 1003 patients with metastatic disease were analysed here: 502 (50%) allocated to standard treatment (SOC-alone group)..."

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The primary outcome measure was overall survival.
Primary Endpoint(s).plan_extraction_plan: The primary endpoint is "overall survival". This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 3, in the sentence: "The primary outcome measure was overall survival." It is also reiterated on page 4, in the "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section (2.4), in the sentence: "The primary outcome for this comparison was overall survival, with secondary outcomes of failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, skeletal-related events, disease-specific survival, toxicity and therapy for progression."

Quality of Life Scale.plan_extraction_plan: The PDF mentions "QOL, quality of life" in the abbreviations section (page 1, page 3) and discusses "patient-related QoL outcomes" in the discussion (page 10), referencing a previous STAMPEDE trial comparison (Reference 14). However, this PDF does not specify the particular standardized questionnaire or survey used as the "Quality of Life Scale" for assessing participants' overall well-being. It states that QoL data was not updated in this report.

Quality of Life reported.plan_extraction_plan: The "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section (2.4) on page 4 does not list Quality of Life as a primary or secondary outcome for this specific analysis. While the abbreviation QOL is defined on page 1 and previous STAMPEDE analyses are mentioned to have supported the use of abiraterone over docetaxel based on patient-related QoL outcomes (page 10, text), this paper explicitly states "No additional toxicity data has been collected since the primary report in 2017, so we have not updated those aspects here" (page 10, text), and QoL is not presented in any of the results tables or figures.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.plan_extraction_plan: While the trial population included a high proportion of newly diagnosed (synchronous) metastatic patients (94% as per Table 1, page 5), the results are not reported by synchronous versus metachronous as distinct prognostic groups. The reporting is for the overall metastatic population and then stratified by "metastatic disease risk group" (low-risk/high-risk), which is a different classification.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.plan_extraction_plan: The trial population included relapsing (metachronous) patients (6% as per Table 1, page 5), but the results are not reported by synchronous versus metachronous as distinct prognostic groups. The reporting is for the overall metastatic population and then stratified by "metastatic disease risk group" (low-risk/high-risk), which is a different classification.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The results are reported based on a "high-risk" metastatic disease group, which is defined by criteria commonly associated with high volume/burden of metastases (≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8, as stated in the "2.3 Procedures" section on page 4). Table 3 on page 8 presents "Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria" with a dedicated column for "High-risk".
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.plan_extraction_plan: The results are reported based on a "high-risk" metastatic disease group, which is defined by criteria commonly associated with high volume/burden of metastases (≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8, as stated in the "2.3 Procedures" section on page 4). Table 3 on page 8 presents "Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria" with a dedicated column for "High-risk". The Abstract on page 3 also mentions "metastatic disease risk group: 48% high".

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The results are reported based on a "low-risk" metastatic disease group, which represents patients not meeting the criteria for high-risk disease (as defined in the "2.3 Procedures" section on page 4). Table 3 on page 8 presents "Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria" with a dedicated column for "Low-risk".
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.plan_extraction_plan: The results are reported based on a "low-risk" metastatic disease group, which represents patients not meeting the criteria for high-risk disease (as defined in the "2.3 Procedures" section on page 4). Table 3 on page 8 presents "Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria" with a dedicated column for "Low-risk". The Abstract on page 3 also mentions "metastatic disease risk group: 44% low".

Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The primary outcome for this comparison was overall survival, with secondary outcomes of failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, skeletal-related events, disease-specific survival, toxicity and therapy for progression.
Secondary Endpoint(s).plan_extraction_plan: The secondary endpoints are explicitly stated in the "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section (Section 2.4) on page 4, and also listed in Table 2 on page 6.

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The specific outcome measure "Time To Progression of Serum Androgen" (TTPSA) is not reported in the PDF. The document reports "Progression-free survival" (PFS) which is a broader composite endpoint that includes PSA progression, but also radiological or clinical progression. A value specifically for "Time To Progression of Serum Androgen" is not provided for the treatment arm (SOC + AAP).

TTPSA (mo) | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The specific outcome measure "Time To Progression of Serum Androgen" (TTPSA) is not reported in the PDF. The document reports "Progression-free survival" (PFS) which is a broader composite endpoint that includes PSA progression, but also radiological or clinical progression. A value specifically for "Time To Progression of Serum Androgen" is not provided for the control arm (SOC-alone).

Total Participants - N.evidence: One thousand and three patients were contemporaneously randomised (November 2011 to January 2014)...
Total Participants - N.plan_extraction_plan: In the abstract, locate the sentence: "One thousand and three patients were contemporaneously randomised (November 2011 to January 2014)..." and extract the number "1003".

Treatment Arm - N.plan_extraction_plan: In Figure 1, locate the box "Allocated to SOC+AAP" and extract the 'N' value. In Table 1, locate the column header "SOC + AAP" and extract the 'n' value directly below it. Both sources confirm the number of participants in the SOC + AAP arm is 501.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.plan_extraction_plan: The regimen for Treatment Arm 1 (the experimental arm) is reported in the abstract and the methods section. Extract the regimen described for the "SOC + AAP" group. The abstract states: "SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP)". Also, extract the regimen described for the "SOC + AAP" group. It states: "Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)".

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE
Treatment Arm(s).plan_extraction_plan: The experimental treatment arm is "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP)". This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 2: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE". Further details are provided on page 3 in the abstract: "abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP)".

Treatment Class.value: Standard-of-care (SOC) / androgen deprivation therapy (ADT); Standard-of-care (SOC) + Abiraterone acetate + Prednisolone (AAP)
Treatment Class.plan_extraction_plan: Extract the treatment arms described in the "Abstract" and "METHODS" sections. The abstract states: "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." and "The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP), continued until disease progression." Value: Standard-of-care (SOC) / androgen deprivation therapy (ADT); Standard-of-care (SOC) + Abiraterone acetate + Prednisolone (AAP)

Trial.evidence: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patientsstarting hormone therapy: 5-year follow-up results from theSTAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)
Trial.plan_extraction_plan: The trial name is "STAMPEDE". It is explicitly mentioned in the title of the article on page 1: "STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)". It is also frequently referenced throughout the text, for example, in the abstract on page 3: "Initial results from the STAMPEDE trial for advanced prostate cancer showed that adding abiraterone acetate and prednisone (AAP) to androgen deprivation therapy improved progression-free survival."

Type of Therapy.value: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP), androgen deprivation therapy (ADT), gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy
Type of Therapy.evidence: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE, a multiarm, multistage platform trial in men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer. "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." "All patients received lifelong ADT using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)."
Type of Therapy.plan_extraction_plan: On page 3, in the Abstract section, extract the sentence "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE, a multiarm, multistage platform trial in men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer." and "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." Also, in the "2.3 Procedures" section, extract "All patients received lifelong ADT using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)."

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants in the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) with high volume of disease (classified as "High-risk" using LATITUDE criteria) is reported in Table 1, under the "SOC + AAP" column, row "High-risk".

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants in the control arm (SOC-alone) with high volume of disease (classified as "High-risk" using LATITUDE criteria) is reported in Table 1, under the "SOC-alone" column, row "High-risk".

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants in the control arm (SOC-alone) with low volume of disease (classified as "Low-risk" using LATITUDE criteria) is reported in Table 1, under the "SOC-alone" column, row "Low-risk".
